title: ilex wilsonii var. wilsonii
intro: Ilex wilsonii var. wilsonii, also known as Wilson's Holly, is a shrub or small tree native to China, belonging to the holly family (Aquifoliolaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its berries, which contain a compound called ilicin, an alkaloid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Wilson's Holly include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. The herbal preparations of Wilson's Holly typically include the berries, which can be made into teas, infusions, or dried extracts, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
